Skip to contents
ppcviz 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
    • Introduction to ppcviz

Package index

Visualization PIT values

Compute probability integral transform (PIT) values for a specific plot type. Non-uniform PITs indicate the visualization misrepresents the data distribution.

pit_kde()
Compute Visualization PIT Values for KDE
pit_histogram()
Compute Visualization PIT Values for a Histogram
pit_qdotplot()
Compute Randomised PIT Values for Quantile Dot Plots

Goodness-of-fit tests

Test whether PIT values are consistent with the uniform distribution using simultaneous ECDF confidence bands.

viz_gof()
Goodness-of-Fit Test for Visualization PIT Values
check_viz()
Convenience Wrapper: Check Whether a Visualization is Appropriate

Data property detection

Detect discreteness, point masses, and natural boundaries in observed data before choosing a plot type.

detect_discrete()
Detect Discreteness or Point Masses in Data
detect_bounds()
Detect Natural Bounds in Data

Calibration plots

PAVA-based calibration plots for binary and discrete outcomes. Not available in bayesplot.

ppc_calibration()
PAVA Calibration Plot for Binary Outcomes
ppc_calibration_residual()
PAVA Calibration Residual Plot
ppc_calibration_discrete()
PAVA Calibration Plot for Discrete / Multinomial Outcomes

Automatic recommender

Data-driven recommendation of the most appropriate PPC plot function.

recommend_ppc()
Automatic PPC Plot Recommender

On this page
